topic: A person using a 'Self-Checkout' machine at a grocery store.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: The shopper scans the first item at the self-checkout — front view
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: The screen displays the item total as the shopper confirms payment — close-up
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: The shopper taps payment, collects the receipt, and bags the item — side-angle
parsed_generation.narration: A single shopper uses a self-checkout, scanning items, confirming totals on the screen, and completing payment in a tight, cinematic 8-second sequence. The shots move from a front view to a close-up of the screen and finally a side-angle of the payment and bagging.

feedback: The output stays on a single shopper at a self-checkout and uses clear shot transitions, but it diverges from the gold standard in timestamp structure and action granularity. To improve alignment, structure the sequence into two 4-second segments, emphasize key actions (beep after scanning, bagging area, card tap, receipt printing), and tune the camera angles to include a wider establishing shot and more explicit close-ups. Adopting a more cinematic verb-set (e.g., 'beeps', 'slides item into bagging area') will tighten the prompt style toward the gold standard.
strengths: Maintains a single-subject focus around the self-checkout interaction.
Uses concise, action-first language with clear shot labels and camera-angle cues.
fixes: Adopt the gold-standard two-segment structure with [00:00-00:04] and [00:04-00:08], ensuring actions map to each segment (scanning with beep, bagging area; screen update, card tap, receipt).
Increase visual specificity by including more camera angles (wide establishing shot, macro close-up on the screen, tracking on the card tap) and stronger cinematic verbs.
